title: FULL Guide to Becoming a Principled Agentic Engineer (Build Anything with AI)
summary: Cole Medin presents a three-phase agentic engineering workflow: Planning (turning ideas into PRDs and tickets automatically), the PIV Loop (Plan-Implement-Validate cycle per ticket), and System Evolution (using bugs to improve the AI layer system-wide). The methodology is designed to be lightweight and composable with existing development practices.
writeup: A one-hour workshop distilling a three-phase framework for reliable AI-assisted software development: ideation with coding agents, the iterative Piv Loop for executing individual tickets, and a system evolution mindset for making the agent stack progressively more capable. The presenter argues against over-engineered off-the-shelf frameworks like BMad or GSD in favor of a minimal, moldable foundation, demonstrated live with Claude Code and Jira on a brownfield application. The through-line is that the engineer's role shifts from writing code to the higher-leverage work of planning, reducing assumptions, and validating output.
key_points: The Piv Loop is the core unit of work: a repeatable, per-ticket iteration where the developer plans with the coding agent, delegates implementation, then validates the result rather than writing code directly.

Most coding-agent failures are not broken code but misaligned code; the fix is a structured clarification stage before any generation, where the agent is explicitly instructed to ask questions for 20-30 minutes until assumptions are minimized.

Off-the-shelf AI coding frameworks (BMad, GSD, GitHub SpecKit, Cloud Flow) embed sound engineering ideas but are too opinionated and bloated to fit most teams' existing workflows; the presenter's approach is deliberately minimal so it can be grafted onto existing sprint and QA processes.

The planning phase is tool-agnostic: one work-management surface (Jira, GitHub Issues, Linear) plus one LLM coding assistant is the only required stack, with the demo pairing Claude Code and Jira purely as the presenter's current preference.

An open-source companion repository in the presenter's GitHub holds the full reusable system, including Claude Code slash commands, skills, and rules stored in the .claude folder, so the workflow machinery is version-controlled and portable rather than held in muscle memory.

chapters.title: Planning Phase: Brain Dump and Clarification
